questions.question: Explain the concept of tissue organization in multicellular organisms.
questions.answer: In multicellular organisms similar cells group together to form tissues. Tissues combine to form organs and organ systems, which improves efficiency and coordination in body functions.

questions.question: Differentiate between plant tissues and animal tissues.
questions.answer: Plant tissues mainly provide support and transport substances, whereas animal tissues are specialized for movement and coordination.

questions.question: Describe the structure and function of meristematic tissue.
questions.answer: Meristematic tissues consist of actively dividing cells with dense cytoplasm and prominent nucleus. They help in plant growth.

questions.question: Explain the types of meristematic tissues with examples.
questions.answer: Meristematic tissues include apical meristem, lateral meristem and intercalary meristem which help increase length and girth of plants.

questions.question: Explain the structure and function of parenchyma.
questions.answer: Parenchyma cells are living cells with thin walls that store food and help in photosynthesis.

questions.question: Describe collenchyma tissue and its role in plants.
questions.answer: Collenchyma provides support and flexibility to plant stems and leaves.

questions.question: Describe sclerenchyma tissue with its functions.
questions.answer: Sclerenchyma cells are dead with thick lignified walls providing strength to plants.

questions.question: Explain complex permanent tissues with examples.
questions.answer: Complex permanent tissues consist of different cell types working together such as xylem and phloem.

questions.question: Explain the structure and functions of xylem.
questions.answer: Xylem transports water and minerals and provides mechanical support.

questions.question: Explain the structure and functions of phloem.
questions.answer: Phloem transports food from leaves to different parts of the plant.

questions.question: Explain epidermal tissue in plants.
questions.answer: Epidermis forms the outer protective layer and prevents water loss.

questions.question: Describe stomata and their function.
questions.answer: Stomata regulate gas exchange and transpiration in plants.

questions.question: Explain epithelial tissue with its functions.
questions.answer: Epithelial tissue protects the body and lines internal organs.

questions.question: Describe connective tissue with examples.
questions.answer: Connective tissue connects and supports organs. Examples include bone, cartilage and blood.

questions.question: Explain bone tissue and its functions.
questions.answer: Bone tissue provides structure, protects organs and enables movement.

questions.question: Explain cartilage tissue.
questions.answer: Cartilage is a flexible connective tissue that reduces friction in joints.

questions.question: Describe adipose tissue and its importance.
questions.answer: Adipose tissue stores fat and insulates the body.

questions.question: Explain muscular tissue.
questions.answer: Muscular tissue contracts and relaxes to produce movement.

questions.question: Describe the three types of muscle tissue.
questions.answer: The three types are striated muscles, smooth muscles and cardiac muscles.

questions.question: Explain cardiac muscles.
questions.answer: Cardiac muscles are involuntary muscles found only in the heart.

questions.question: Explain nervous tissue.
questions.answer: Nervous tissue transmits impulses and coordinates body functions.

questions.question: Describe the structure of a neuron.
questions.answer: A neuron consists of cell body, dendrites and axon.

questions.question: Explain the function of dendrites.
questions.answer: Dendrites receive impulses from other neurons.

questions.question: Explain the function of axon.
questions.answer: Axon carries impulses away from the cell body.

questions.question: Explain how different tissues work together in animals.
questions.answer: Different tissues combine to form organs and organ systems that perform complex functions.
